title: Daily Market Digest: May 5, 2026
subtitle: A professional financial journalist's daily market summary.

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Edge Higher, S&P 500 Nears Record Amid Strong Earnings and Easing Oil Prices
items.summary: U.S. stock markets moved closer to record levels today, driven by a combination of robust corporate earnings and a cool-off in oil prices, despite ongoing geopolitical concerns in the Middle East. The overall market sentiment remains cautiously optimistic as investors balance positive economic data with central bank policy uncertainties and international tensions.

items.statContext: Nearing or reaching a new all-time high of 7255 points, buoyed by tech stocks.
Heading toward record territory, lifted by technology sector strength.
Positive movement, with Caterpillar leading gains.
Small-cap index leading among major U.S. indexes, indicating strength in smaller companies.

items.headline: Pinterest Soars on Strong User Growth and Earnings Beat
items.summary: Pinterest ($PINS) jumped 14% (pre-market +15.97%) after beating profit and revenue estimates, driven by an 11% rise in monthly active users and the fastest sales growth since Q2 2024.

items.headline: Sterling Infrastructure Leads Pre-Market Gains
items.summary: Sterling Infrastructure, Inc. ($STRL) was a notable pre-market gainer.

items.headline: Intel Jumps on Apple Talks and AI Confidence
items.summary: Intel ($INTC) surged over 13% in pre-market trading, fueled by reports of preliminary discussions with Apple for chip manufacturing and increased investor confidence in its AI-linked turnaround strategy.

items.headline: DuPont Exceeds Expectations
items.summary: DuPont ($DD) saw an 8.7% increase after reporting better-than-expected quarterly results and raising its full-year outlook.

items.headline: Shopify Falls Despite Earnings Beat, Guidance Weighs
items.summary: Shopify ($SHOP) shares dropped approximately 7% after the company, despite beating earnings, provided guidance that disappointed investors.

items.headline: AMD Shares Decline
items.summary: Advanced Micro Devices ($AMD) experienced a decline.

items.headline: Graco Inc. Among Pre-Market Losers
items.summary: Graco Inc. ($GGG) was among the top pre-market losers.

items.headline: Visa Experiences Decline
items.summary: Visa ($V) posted a loss, contributing to the Dow's mixed performance.

items.headline: Key Economic Data and Geopolitical Developments Shape Investor Outlook
items.summary: The ISM Services PMI was released at 53.6, meeting expectations, with its employment component at 48.0. Upcoming economic releases include the U.S. trade deficit, the Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey (JOLTS), and the crucial Non-Farm Payrolls report on Friday. Geopolitical tensions, particularly concerning the U.S.-Iran conflict and the Strait of Hormuz, continue to influence oil prices, though equities have largely remained resilient.

items.headline: Strong Q1 Earnings Drive Market Resilience
items.summary: Several major companies reported earnings today. DuPont, Pinterest, AB InBev, and Pfizer all exceeded expectations, contributing to the positive market sentiment. Conversely, Shopify saw a decline despite an earnings beat due to cautious guidance. Palantir ($PLTR) also fell in pre-market despite revenue growth, and Vertex Pharmaceuticals ($VRTX) showed mixed results.

items.headline: Technology and Small-Caps Lead the Market
items.summary: Technology stocks were instrumental in buoying the S&P 500 and Nasdaq today, highlighting continued investor interest in the sector. Small-cap stocks, as reflected by the Russell 2000's strong performance, also showed significant leadership, suggesting broader market participation. Investments in AI infrastructure continue to be a key theme.

items.headline: SEC Proposes Semiannual Reporting Option for Companies
items.summary: The Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has introduced a proposal that would allow companies to opt for semiannual reporting.
